trackGuide:
Sector 1: Turns 1–6
✅
Turn 1 (The Hilltop)
Approach:
Turn 1 is a steep uphill braking zone that demands precise car control. Brake at the 100m mark or just before, depending on your cars braking capabilities.
Tip:
A smooth entry is key here, and you should carry a lot of speed through this corner. Avoid late braking or you might run wide over the crest of the hill.
✅
Turn 2 (Left-Right Chicane)
Approach:
As you crest Turn 1, its important to set up properly for Turn 2, which is a quick left-right chicane. A little lift or a light brake will help you keep control through the left-hander.
Tip:
Avoid too much curb usage; it can unsettle the car, especially through the right-hander.
✅
Turn 3 (Medium-Speed Right-Hander)
Approach:
Turn 3 is a medium-speed right-hander that requires steady throttle control. Aim to brake before the 50m mark and carry a smooth, consistent line through the corner.
Tip:
Make sure youre not too aggressive on the throttle as it could cause the rear end to slide.
✅
Turn 4 (The High-Speed Left-Hander)
Approach:
Turn 4 is a high-speed left-hander. The key here is precision — dont brake too much, but make sure youre setting up for the next part of the track.
Tip:
Its best to carry a lot of speed through this corner as it leads into a long sequence of turns.
✅
Turn 5-6 (The Right-Left Combo)
Approach:
Turns 5 and 6 form a sharp right-left sequence. Turn 5 is a tight right that leads to a long, sweeping left-hander in Turn 6.
Tip:
Brake early and let the car rotate naturally through Turn 5, keeping it balanced for Turn 6.
Sector 2: Turns 7–11
✅
Turn 7 (High-Speed Right-Hander)
Approach:
Turn 7 is a high-speed right-hander. This is an easy flat-out corner if you have a stable car setup.
Tip:
Ensure your car is stable here, as youre about to enter another tough sequence.
✅
Turn 8–9 (The Double-Left)
Approach:
Turns 8 and 9 form a tight double left. Youll need to brake lightly to set up for the left-hander, but be sure to balance the car so it flows nicely between these two corners.
Tip:
Avoid aggressive throttle inputs through these corners to prevent oversteer.
✅
Turn 10 (The Hard Braking Zone)
Approach:
Turn 10 is a heavy braking zone. Brake hard before the 100m board and downshift to 2nd gear. The corner is tight, so maintaining balance and getting a clean exit is crucial.
Tip:
Avoid running too wide on the exit, as it will cost you speed in the next straight.
✅
Turn 11 (Tight Left-Hander)
Approach:
This is a tight left-hand corner after a fast section. It requires quick throttle control to avoid wheelspin.
Tip:
Brake just before the 50m mark and try to keep a steady pace to set up for the high-speed section coming up.
Sector 3: Turns 12–20
✅
Turn 12 (The Fast Left-Hander)
Approach:
Turn 12 is one of the fastest corners on the circuit, taken at nearly full throttle. The key is to keep the car as stable as possible and position it correctly for Turn 13.
Tip:
Keep the car as flat as possible and avoid any sudden steering corrections.
✅
Turn 13 (Another Tight Right)
Approach:
This is a medium-speed right-hander, so brake just before the 50m board and make sure you’ve got a solid line through the apex.
Tip:
Keep the throttle smooth on the exit to avoid unnecessary wheelspin.
✅
Turn 14-15 (Double Right)
Approach:
Turn 14 is a sharp right, while Turn 15 is a faster right that opens up. Brake at the 50m mark and try to keep a nice rhythm as you flow through the two corners.
Tip:
Carry as much speed as possible without compromising the car’s balance.
✅
Turn 16 (The Left-Hander)
Approach:
A fairly simple left-hander that allows you to focus on getting the best run into the final corner.
Tip:
It’s an easy corner, but don’t allow yourself to be too relaxed here as it leads to the start-finish straight.
✅
Turn 17-18 (The Final Chicane)
Approach:
The final corners are a tight right-left combination that leads to the main straight. Brake before the 100m board, aiming for a precise entry into both corners.
Tip:
Keep the car balanced and try to maximize your exit speed as you prepare for the long run down to Turn 1.
Car
drivingTips:
Driving Tips
✅
Smooth Inputs:
COTA requires a controlled and smooth driving style. Sudden throttle or steering inputs can unsettle the car, especially in the high-speed corners.
✅
Tire Management:
The rear tires can overheat, especially during long high-speed corners. Be mindful of your throttle application to prevent losing grip.
✅
Braking:
Practice your braking zones carefully. There are several heavy braking zones where lock-ups are common, especially into Turns 10 and 12.
Conclusion
The
Circuit of the Americas
rewards precision, consistency, and car control. If you can master the high-speed sections and ensure you’re managing your braking zones effectively, you’ll be on your way to consistently fast lap times. Focus on getting clean exits, especially out of the tight corners like Turn 10 and Turn 13, and remember that smoothness is key to maximizing your performance throughout the lap.
Circuit of the Americas race strategy
COTA
